methodology: Refactored to Empowered-style OKRs: a small set of company objectives, outcome-oriented key results, explicit team ownership, and limited use of High Integrity Commitments.
description: Preparation Objectives for the next quarter.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe meaningful business and customer outcomes, not feature delivery.
Key results are assigned to a small set of company objectives rather than standing alone as disconnected persona goals.
Every key result names executing and supporting teams so accountability is explicit.
High Integrity Commitments are used sparingly and only for work that is materially deadline-sensitive.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Make execution reliably repeatable
companyObjectives.statement: Improve the operating system behind the product so launches, service recovery, reliability, and cross-functional delivery become predictably repeatable.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Keeps teams accountable for operational outcomes and avoids turning roadmap delivery into a checklist of feature shipments.

keyResults.baseline: Baseline to be confirmed from quarterly reporting
keyResults.statement: Increase Customer adoption and satisfaction during the quarter.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve customer adoption and satisfaction.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for customer adoption and satisfaction, aligned with its focus on own shared data products, reporting, operational telemetry, and partner integration foundations. This team specifically focuses on cell 2.

Support the KR by contributing capability for customer adoption and satisfaction, aligned with its focus on own shared data products, reporting, operational telemetry, and partner integration foundations. This team specifically focuses on cell 3.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

keyResults.baseline: Baseline to be confirmed from quarterly reporting
keyResults.statement: Increase Service reliability during the quarter.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve service reliability.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for service reliability, aligned with its focus on own payment processing, fraud controls, settlement, and identity capabilities shared across DKV products. This team specifically focuses on cell 1.

Support the KR by contributing capability for service reliability, aligned with its focus on own shared data products, reporting, operational telemetry, and partner integration foundations. This team specifically focuses on cell 1.

Support the KR by contributing capability for service reliability, aligned with its focus on own fuel-to-charging transition products, roaming, and energy-service propositions. This team specifically focuses on cell 2.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Turn product progress into economic leverage
companyObjectives.statement: Translate better customer and operational outcomes into stronger revenue quality, retention, and strategic leverage for the business.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Separates commercial results from activity metrics so leaders can evaluate whether product work is compounding into business value.
